title: agent-remember: project-scoped persistent memory primitive
body:
# Goal

Project-scoped persistent memory primitive: short
declarative facts (operational rules, codebase folklore,
architectural decisions) that travel with the planner data
and surface at session start.

End state: `jjf remember "always rebuild before mutating
verbs"` writes a memory keyed by an auto-generated slug;
`jjf memories` lists them; `jjf recall <key>` reads one;
`jjf forget <key>` removes one. Memories ride the `issues`
bookmark via `jjf push` / `jjf pull` like everything else.

# Why now

beads (see `reference/beads/cmd/bd/memory.go`) shows the
shape: a key/value store of short insights, surfaced via
`bd prime` at session start. It's the closest beads has to
a per-project agent-context primitive. Today jjforge has no
equivalent — operational rules live in `CLAUDE.md` (per-tree,
not synced with planner data) or in Claude Code's
`~/.claude/projects/<dir>/memory/` (per-user, not shared
across operators).

A jjforge-native version closes the gap: rules a subagent
discovers ("the colocate drift trick"; "use the dual-file
filter") travel with the planner; every operator who pulls
the `issues` bookmark inherits them automatically. Replaces
the "did I remember to write that down in CLAUDE.md" workflow
with "every machine sees the same memories."

# Scope

## Storage

- New on-bookmark file family: `memories/<key>.json`, one
  file per memory.
- Schema:
  ```json
  {
    "key": "<kebab-slug>",
    "value": "<the insight>",
    "created_at": "<rfc3339>",
    "updated_at": "<rfc3339>"
  }
  ```
- New `Op::SetMemory { key, value }` and `Op::UnsetMemory
  { key }` trailers. Same multi-op commit machinery as
  issues.
- Memory keys are last-write-wins under the existing
  op-space resolver (scalar policy).
- `jjf forget` writes an `unset-memory` op; the storage
  layer treats unset like cleared scalar (file deleted,
  history preserved).

### Slug generation

Port `slugify()` from `beads/cmd/bd/memory.go:23-44`: lowercase,
non-alphanumeric → hyphen, first ~8 hyphen-separated tokens,
cap at 60 chars. Operator can override with `--key <slug>`.

### Storage API

```rust
impl Storage {
    pub fn set_memory(&self, key: &str, value: &str) -> Result<()>;
    pub fn unset_memory(&self, key: &str) -> Result<()>;
    pub fn list_memories(&self) -> Result<Vec<Memory>>;
    pub fn read_memory(&self, key: &str) -> Result<Option<Memory>>;
}
```

## CLI

- `jjf remember "<insight>"` — write; key auto-slugged.
- `jjf remember "<insight>" --key <slug>` — write with
  explicit key.
- `jjf remember --key <slug> -F -` — write value from
  stdin/file (for multi-line insights).
- `jjf memories` — list (key + truncated value).
- `jjf memories <substring>` — filter (case-insensitive).
- `jjf recall <key>` — print full value to stdout.
- `jjf forget <key>` — remove.
- All verbs accept `--json`.

Output for `jjf memories` plain-text:

```
<key>
  <value truncated to 120 chars>
```

Output for `jjf memories --json`: array of `{key, value,
created_at, updated_at}` objects.

## Surfacing at session start

Lightweight: extend `jjf show roadmap` (i.e. `jjf show
9566f52`) with `--include-memories` that appends a
`## Persistent Memories` block after the body, formatted
like beads' `prime` output (`reference/beads/cmd/bd/prime.go:333-393`).

Heavier (follow-up, not this ticket): a dedicated
`jjf prime` verb that bundles roadmap + memories + ready
in one call. Sketch only; defer to a separate ticket if
the lightweight version proves insufficient.

## Spec

- `docs/storage-format.md`:
  - New §X "Memories" section: file family, schema, slug rules.
  - §5.2: add `set-memory`, `unset-memory` to the op
    vocab.
- `docs/cli-json.md`: new envelope shapes for the four
  memory verbs.

# Acceptance

- [ ] `jjf remember "X"` round-trips: `jjf recall <auto-slug>` returns "X".
- [ ] `jjf remember "X" --key explicit-key` round-trips.
- [ ] `jjf remember "X' --key k`, then `jjf remember "Y" --key k`: `recall k` returns "Y" (upsert).
- [ ] `jjf forget k`: subsequent `recall k` returns nothing (exit 1 in plain mode).
- [ ] `jjf memories` lists every set memory.
- [ ] `jjf memories foo` filters by substring on key OR value.
- [ ] `jjf push` / `jjf pull` round-trips the memories file family.
- [ ] `jjf show roadmap --include-memories` appends the memories block.
- [ ] Storage tests for `set_memory` / `unset_memory` /
  `list_memories` / `read_memory`.
- [ ] CLI integration tests for each verb.
- [ ] Spec docs updated.
- [ ] `cargo nextest run --workspace` stays green.

# Out of scope

- A dedicated `jjf prime` verb (follow-up).
- Hooks that auto-inject memories into CLAUDE Code session
  start (operator-side, separate concern).
- Memory categories / namespaces (`memory.dolt-phantoms`
  vs `memory.workflow-rules`). v1 is flat.
- TTL / decay / "forget after N days." If wanted, a
  separate ticket once usage patterns are clear.
- Per-issue memories. v1 is global to the bookmark.
- A memory-history surfacer ("when was this memory set,
  by whom"). Op trailers carry it; surfacing is a follow-up.

# Pointers

- Reference impl: `reference/beads/cmd/bd/memory.go`
  (four verbs, slugify, upsert) and
  `reference/beads/cmd/bd/prime.go:332-393` (formatting).
- Storage precedent: how `Op::SetType` / `Op::SetSlug`
  flow through the multi-op commit pattern (`crates/jjf-storage/src/op.rs`).
- Resolver: scalar LWW from `crates/jjf-storage/src/merge_ops.rs`
  applies directly — memories are scalars keyed by slug.
- Slug validation precedent: `crates/jjf-storage/src/id.rs`
  (kebab-case rules already used for issue slugs).
